statute: New Jersey Statutes § 56:8-136 et seq. (Contractors' Registration Act); N.J.A.C. 13:45A (Consumer Affairs)
license_label: NJ Home Improvement Contractor Registration Number
license_notes: Home improvement contractors must register with NJ Division of Consumer Affairs. Commercial contractors check locally. Financed home repair contracts require separate Home Repair Contractor License from NJ Dept. of Banking and Insurance. Electrical, plumbing, HVAC require state licenses.

mandatory_clauses.label: NJ HIC registration disclosure
3-day right to cancel
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL: You may cancel this contract within three business days of signing. To cancel, send written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Deposit limitation
mandatory_clauses.text: DEPOSIT: The deposit collected under this contract shall not exceed one-third (1/3) of the total contract price. Deposit amount: ${{deposit_amount}}. Total contract price: ${{contract_price}}.

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: 1st offense civil penalty up to $10,000; subsequent up to $20,000 per offense. Criminal charges possible.
notes: NJ requires HIC registration for home improvement work on residential property. Written contract required for jobs over $25. Deposit cap: 1/3 of contract price. 3-day cancellation right. Financed home repair contracts need separate DOBI license. Electrical, plumbing, HVAC require state licenses through Division of Consumer Affairs. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements (NJ waives state notice for contracts at seller's office).
